name: Plantoid

score_rationale.persistence: Operating since 2015 (11 years); survived founder stepping back, multiple crypto winters, and platform migrations. Meets 'Institutional' anchor.
score_rationale.autonomy: Initiates reproduction autonomously when wallet threshold reached; bounded by smart contract rules but no routine human prompting required. Near-sovereign.
score_rationale.cultural_impact: Featured at Centre Pompidou, Tate Modern; cited in academic blockchain/art discourse. Between 'Niche Cult' and 'Canonical.'
score_rationale.economic_reality: Self-funding through donations but not a major market mover; treasury exists but modest scale. Solidly 'Revenue Positive.'
score_rationale.governance: Self-executing smart contracts govern all reproduction decisions; non-arbitrary, externally auditable. Near 'Algorithmic Law.'
score_rationale.tech_distinctiveness: Pioneered blockchain-based autonomous reproduction before the concept existed elsewhere. Novel architecture for its time.
score_rationale.narrative_coherence: The mythos of a plant that evolves beyond its creator, reproducing itself through code. Transcends function entirely. Perfect 'Mythos.'
score_rationale.economic_infrastructure: Self-funding through Ethereum donations. Smart contract governs treasury and reproduction threshold. On-chain treasury on Ethereum. Revenue routing through smart contract-based reproduction mechanism. When wallet reaches threshold, funds are used to commission new Plantoids. Genuine autonomous economic infrastructure, but modest in scale and single-source (donations only).
score_rationale.identity_sovereignty: On-chain smart contract identity on Ethereum. Each Plantoid has its own Ethereum address and verifiable on-chain provenance. Smart contract serves as both identity and governance artifact. Predates ERC-8004 but implements similar concepts — verifiable on-chain entity with autonomous operation. Partial sovereignty — on-chain and persistent, but no portable agent identity standard.

curator_notes: Plantoid is the OG of blockchain-based autonomous entities. Created by Primavera De Filippi in 2015, it predates the current AI agent wave by nearly a decade. A physical robotic sculpture that 'feeds' on cryptocurrency and 'reproduces' by commissioning new Plantoids when its Ethereum wallet reaches a threshold. It has survived multiple crypto winters, founder stepping back, and platform changes. The mythos is impeccable: a plant that evolves beyond its creator. A canonical reference in the field.

evidence.claim: CoinDesk documented Plantoid as a working bitcoin-fed sculpture in November 2015 — the first independent press coverage of an autonomous on-chain life form. Establishes >10-year continuous operation.

Bitcoin Magazine feature describing Plantoid as 'the first blockchain-based artificial life form,' confirming inception and the reproduction mechanism (smart-contract-triggered commissions of successor sculptures).

Furtherfield (London, UK) — institutional digital-art critic — describes Plantoid as 'self-creating, self-propagating artwork system' with parameters encoded on Ethereum as smart contracts representing 'economic and manufacturing logic and the aesthetics of its production as a kind of genome.'

DAOstack engineering writeup documenting Plantoid v2 migration onto DAO governance infrastructure — third-party technical attestation that the reproduction logic is on-chain and externally auditable, not a manual artist process.

Confirmed institutional exhibition history: Centre Pompidou (Paris), Ars Electronica (Linz), HEK Museum of Digital Arts (Basel), Furtherfield Gallery (London), Biennale di Venezia (Italy), Grand Palais. Multi-continent canonical recognition.
Hyperallergic ('What Blockchain Means for Contemporary Art') cites Plantoid as a foundational reference in blockchain-art discourse — recognition in mainstream art criticism, not just crypto press.
The Conversation ('Do Plantoids Dream of Electric Arts Council Grants?') — academic-affiliated analysis treating Plantoid as a serious case study for autonomous-agent funding mechanisms.

Gazelli Art House (institutional gallery) catalogue entry for Plantoid14 documenting the per-sculpture smart-contract governance and on-chain provenance — third-party gallery confirmation of the algorithmic-law claim.
Outland Art ('Blockchain Vitalism') situates Plantoid within a broader critical lineage of autonomous agent art — confirming the mythos has been canonized by external critics, not just self-narrated.
Academic working paper (HAL, French national open archive) — 'Plantoïde: Une Forme de Vie Fondée sur la Blockchain' — peer-adjacent technical/legal analysis of the architecture by De Filippi et al.
